source_pr.title: test: make `TestMergeCreateTableJobs` stable
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #55753

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

use `slices.SortFunc` to make the result stable

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [x] No need to test
  > - [x] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Test assertions depended on the ordering of `mergeCreateTableJobs(jobWs)` output (`newWs`). The order can vary across runs, causing intermittent failures.
failure_signature: TestMergeCreateTableJobs: flaky due to nondeterministic ordering of `newWs` (mergeCreateTableJobs result) when compared against expected slices
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it compared slices returned by `mergeCreateTableJobs` as if they had a stable order, but the order of merged job wrappers is not guaranteed to be deterministic. The fix makes the test robust by explicitly sorting `newWs` (by job type, schema name, table name, etc.) before asserting equality, removing the dependence on incidental iteration/order.
fix_pattern: Stabilize tests by sorting non-deterministically ordered slices before assertions (avoid relying on incidental iteration/order of returned collections).
analysis:

Triage summary: slices.SortFunc(newWs, func(a, b *JobWrapper) int { (pkg/ddl/executor_nokit_test.go)
Changed files (1): pkg/ddl/executor_nokit_test.go
Patch url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/55774.patch
Picked test: type=unit path=pkg/ddl/executor_nokit_test.go name=TestMergeCreateTableJobs
